message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this plan through ONE lens. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Check /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board and /home/polaris/wt/flywheel.

PLAN DIAGNOSIS: undefined
RETRIEVAL: undefined
SYNTHESIS LANE: undefined
COMPOSER: undefined
EXPECTED: undefined

LENS:
JUDGE-INVISIBILITY: RACE deletes all [n] markers and the bibliography, then scores our report side-by-side against a 0.5102 reference in ONE call. Would the judge SEE this lever, and would it push a criterion into the top band?

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
12:   - Scorer: **RACE** — reference-based LLM judge, 4 dimensions with per-task DYNAMIC weights:
13-     Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability.
14-     Compares the target report against a high-quality REFERENCE report.
15-   - Download: `github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench` — tasks at
16-     `data/prompt_data/query.jsonl` (100 tasks); HF dataset `muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Dataset`.
17:   - Leaderboard (ChatGPT/Gemini deep-research RACE scores) is on HuggingFace.
18-2. **DeepConsult** — open-ended consulting-style report quality (pairwise/quality judge).
19-3. (also referenced for the agent side: BrowseComp, BFCL, TAU-Bench, MCP Agent Bench.)
20-
21-Related: **DeepResearch Bench II** (arXiv:2601.08536) — rubric-from-expert-report diagnosis
22-(the DRB-II wrapper already stubbed in the compete wheel).
23-
--
25:- **RACE is the correct instrument** — confirms the compete wheel's direction; DeepTRACE (faithfulness)
26-  is a SEPARATE, complementary axis, not the mission's quality axis.
27-- "Beat FS-Researcher" is now RUNNABLE: run POLARIS on the DeepResearch Bench 100 tasks, score with
28:  RACE against the reference reports, compare to FS-Researcher + the ChatGPT/Gemini leaderboard.
29-- The two build wheels serve this end:
30:  - **OUTLINE** → depth + compute-and-prove drives Comprehensiveness + Insight (RACE dims 1-2) and
31:    the faithfulness moat (DeepTRACE) that frontier products lack.
32-  - **COMPOSE** → renders all 329 baskets (current s2s3 iter-7 count; 346 was the stale iter-6 count) fast + faithful so nothing is dropped for latency =
33-    Comprehensiveness + Readability, at full corpus depth.
34-- Open gap: we still have NO FS-Researcher report artifact locally; the benchmark download closes it
35-  (we can run POLARIS on the same 100 tasks and score head-to-head).

content.text:
I inspected both locations. The task arrived with **no plan content** — every field is literally `undefined`:

- `PLAN DIAGNOSIS: undefined`
- `RETRIEVAL: undefined`
- `SYNTHESIS LANE: undefined`
- `COMPOSER: undefined`
- `EXPECTED: undefined`

There is no lever, no mechanism, and no predicted score movement to test against the JUDGE-INVISIBILITY lens. Context I did confirm, so the parent can re-dispatch with a real plan:

- `/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv` — the board is on a 0–100 scale (cellcog-max 55.78 top, grok-deeper-search 41.22 bottom), and `citation_accuracy` / `effective_citations` are `-` for every entry. That is consistent with the lens premise: the RACE call sees no `[n]` markers and no bibliography, so citation-shaped work is invisible to it by construction.
- `/home/polaris/polaris_project/benchmark_target.md:12-14` — RACE is a reference-based judge over four dimensions with **per-task dynamic weights** (Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability), scoring the target against a reference report. Any lever must move one of those four bands in a single side-by-side call to count; DeepTRACE/faithfulness is explicitly a separate axis (`benchmark_target.md:25-26`).

Applying the lens to an empty plan can only produce a vacuous "not visible" — the honest verdict under the stated default is refuted.

input.input:
{"refuted": true, "reasoning": "REFUTED — the plan is empty. Every field handed to me (PLAN DIAGNOSIS, RETRIEVAL, SYNTHESIS LANE, COMPOSER, EXPECTED) is the literal string 'undefined'. There is no lever, no mechanism, and no expected score delta to hold up against the JUDGE-INVISIBILITY lens, so the lens cannot be applied and the standing instruction ('Default refuted=true if uncertain') controls.

What I did verify, so the parent can re-dispatch against a real plan:

1. /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv — the GPT-5.5 board is 0-100 scaled (cellcog-max 55.78 top; sourcery 51.17; gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.98; grok-deeper-search 41.22 bottom). Columns are overall_score, comprehensiveness, insight, instruction_following, readability, citation_accuracy, effective_citations — and citation_accuracy/effective_citations are '-' for ALL ten entries. This directly corroborates the lens premise: because RACE strips [n] markers and the bibliography before judging, no citation-shaped work is scoreable in that call.

2. /home/polaris/polaris_project/benchmark_target.md lines 12-14 — RACE is a reference-based LLM judge over exactly four dimensions with per-task DYNAMIC weights: Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, comparing the target report to a high-quality reference. Lines 25-26 further state that DeepTRACE (faithfulness) is a SEPARATE, complementary axis, not the mission's quality axis.

Consequence for the lens: for ANY candidate lever, the bar is that it must (a) survive marker/bibliography deletion — i.e. leave a trace in the prose the judge actually reads — and (b) push one of the four RACE criteria into the top band in a single side-by-side call against the 0.5102 reference. Levers whose entire mechanism lives in citation density, citation placement, bibliography quality, or retrieval provenance are invisible by construction and cannot move RACE at all. An unstated plan trivially fails (a) and (b) since there is nothing for the judge to see.

I am not refuting a specific mechanism as unsound; I am refuting because there is no mechanism present to evaluate. Re-run this lens with the plan body populated.", "fix": "Re-dispatch this JUDGE-INVISIBILITY lens with the plan body actually populated (PLAN DIAGNOSIS / RETRIEVAL / SYNTHESIS LANE / COMPOSER / EXPECTED). The upstream orchestration step that interpolates those fields emitted the literal string 'undefined' for all five, so the template variables were never bound — fix the caller's variable binding before spawning the lens subagents. When re-run, the lens should test each lever against two gates: (1) does it leave a trace in the prose that survives deletion of all [n] markers and the bibliography, and (2) does that trace plausibly push Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, or Readability into the top band in one side-by-side call vs the 0.5102 reference (per /home/polaris/polaris_project/benchmark_target.md:12-14). Any lever whose value is carried solely by citations is dead on arrival — the leaderboard at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv shows citation_accuracy and effective_citations as '-' for every one of the ten entries."}

input.reasoning:
REFUTED — the plan is empty. Every field handed to me (PLAN DIAGNOSIS, RETRIEVAL, SYNTHESIS LANE, COMPOSER, EXPECTED) is the literal string "undefined". There is no lever, no mechanism, and no expected score delta to hold up against the JUDGE-INVISIBILITY lens, so the lens cannot be applied and the standing instruction ("Default refuted=true if uncertain") controls.

What I did verify, so the parent can re-dispatch against a real plan:

1. /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv — the GPT-5.5 board is 0-100 scaled (cellcog-max 55.78 top; sourcery 51.17; gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.98; grok-deeper-search 41.22 bottom). Columns are overall_score, comprehensiveness, insight, instruction_following, readability, citation_accuracy, effective_citations — and citation_accuracy/effective_citations are "-" for ALL ten entries. This directly corroborates the lens premise: because RACE strips [n] markers and the bibliography before judging, no citation-shaped work is scoreable in that call.

2. /home/polaris/polaris_project/benchmark_target.md lines 12-14 — RACE is a reference-based LLM judge over exactly four dimensions with per-task DYNAMIC weights: Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, comparing the target report to a high-quality reference. Lines 25-26 further state that DeepTRACE (faithfulness) is a SEPARATE, complementary axis, not the mission's quality axis.

Consequence for the lens: for ANY candidate lever, the bar is that it must (a) survive marker/bibliography deletion — i.e. leave a trace in the prose the judge actually reads — and (b) push one of the four RACE criteria into the top band in a single side-by-side call against the 0.5102 reference. Levers whose entire mechanism lives in citation density, citation placement, bibliography quality, or retrieval provenance are invisible by construction and cannot move RACE at all. An unstated plan trivially fails both gates, since there is nothing for the judge to see.

I am not refuting a specific mechanism as unsound; I am refuting because there is no mechanism present to evaluate. Re-run this lens with the plan body populated.
input.fix:
Re-dispatch this JUDGE-INVISIBILITY lens with the plan body actually populated (PLAN DIAGNOSIS / RETRIEVAL / SYNTHESIS LANE / COMPOSER / EXPECTED). The upstream orchestration step that interpolates those fields emitted the literal string "undefined" for all five, so the template variables were never bound — fix the caller's variable binding before spawning the lens subagents.

When re-run, the lens should test each lever against two gates: (1) does it leave a trace in the prose that survives deletion of all [n] markers and the bibliography, and (2) does that trace plausibly push Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, or Readability into the top band in one side-by-side call vs the 0.5102 reference (per /home/polaris/polaris_project/benchmark_target.md:12-14). Any lever whose value is carried solely by citations is dead on arrival — the leaderboard at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv shows citation_accuracy and effective_citations as "-" for every one of the ten entries.
